result:
This is an impressive terminal-based life/simulation engine with 177+ simulation modes spanning cellular automata, physics, ecology, chemistry, and more.

**Proposed goal: Add a Deep-Sea Bioluminescent Abyss simulation mode** — model an abyssal ocean zone (1000m+ depth) with anglerfish lure signaling, bioluminescent plankton disturbance cascades (where movement triggers chain-reaction light bursts through nearby organisms), giant squid chromatophore countershading, marine snow particle drift, hydrostatic pressure zones affecting creature metabolism, and predator-prey interactions conducted entirely through light-based deception in total darkness. Three views: dark abyss scene with bioluminescent flashes propagating through the water column, depth-pressure cross-section with creature distribution, and time-series sparklines tracking flash events/predation/plankton density/creature populations. Six presets (Midnight Zone Ecosystem, Anglerfish Hunting Ground, Bioluminescent Storm, Giant Squid Territory, Marine Snow Blizzard, Abyssal Vent Oasis).

This fills a unique niche — the project has tide pools, coral reefs, and aquariums but nothing modeling the deep ocean's light-based ecology in complete darkness, which would make for a visually striking terminal simulation with glowing organisms against a black background.

Shall I build it?
